title: Tailor the Change Process
description: Modify existing workflows and life cycles to support a custom change process. Create object initialization rules to connect workflows with life cycles.

topics.title: Workflow Design and Modification

content.text: Workflows are the engine that drives change objects through their process. Before you can tailor a workflow, you need to understand the anatomy of the out-of-the-box Design Review Workflow and the two types of nodes that control its behavior. Always copy an existing workflow before modifying it - never customize the original OOTB template, because doing so removes your fallback reference and can conflict with future Windchill upgrades.
Anatomy of the Design Review Workflow

The OOTB Design Review Workflow follows a structured sequence of automated and manual steps: (1) A robot assigns the Submitter role, (2) the submitter can choose Submit Later (creating a manual task) or Submit Now (advancing to Review state), (3) participants are assigned to the Review Task, (4) a robot sets the submission date, (5) the Perform Review activity spans the entire review process, (6) a robot locks associated annotations, (7) the Design Review is set to the Approval state and tasks are sent to Approvers, (8) an optional auxiliary review is available, (9) the review results in approval or rejection, and (10) a robot sets the resolution date.
Activity Nodes vs. Robot Nodes

rows: Purpose
Manual tasks completed by assigned roles
Automated actions executed by the system
Visibility
Appears in My Tasks for the assigned user
Does NOT appear in any user's task list
Configuration
Responsible role, template, instructions, participants
Action type, target state, notification settings
Examples
Perform Review, Approve, Decision Required
Set State, Assign Role, Lock Annotations, Set Date
User Interaction
Requires a person to act and complete the task
Runs automatically when the workflow reaches the node

content.text: Never modify the original OOTB workflow template. Always right-click the existing template and use Save As to create a copy first. Modifying the original removes your reference baseline and can cause conflicts during Windchill upgrades.
Modifying the Delta Workflow

When creating the Delta Regulation Review Workflow, you modify four key areas of the copied Design Review Workflow: Set State robots are renamed to match your custom life cycle states (e.g., Set State In Analysis, Set State Pending Approval). Activity nodes are updated with new names, responsible roles, templates, and instructions (e.g., the Approve activity becomes "Decision Required" with Yes/No routing instead of Approve/Reject). Routing events are changed to reflect the new decision options. Messages are updated to reference the correct process names and roles.

content.prompt: Match each workflow modification task to what it accomplishes:

pairs.right: Align automated state changes with custom life cycle states
Assign the correct person to manual workflow tasks

pairs.left: Update routing events from Approve/Reject to Yes/No
pairs.right: Match decision options to the Delta review process
Provide context-specific guidance to task assignees

keyTakeaways: Always copy an existing workflow before modifying it - never edit the original OOTB template directly
Activity nodes represent manual tasks completed by assigned roles and appear in My Tasks; robot nodes are automated actions that run without user interaction
Workflow modifications include renaming Set State robots, updating Activity node roles and instructions, changing routing events, and updating messages
The Delta Regulation Review Workflow changes Approve/Reject routing to Yes/No and assigns the Delta Regulation Manager role

topics.title: Life Cycle Templates

content.text: Life cycle templates define the states and phases that a change object passes through from creation to completion. While workflows control how work gets done at each stage, the life cycle controls what states exist, who can act in each state, and what level of access they have. Together with workflows and team templates, life cycles form the three pillars of the change management system - connected by Object Initialization Rules.
OOTB Design Review Life Cycle

The out-of-the-box Design Review Life Cycle defines six phases: Open, Review, Approval, Approved, Rejected, and Canceled. Each phase represents a distinct state of the review object. The Delta Regulation Review LC modifies this template by changing the Review state to In Analysis, the Approval state to Pending Approval, and adding a new Resolved phase - resulting in seven phases total.

cards.back: The initial phase when the Delta Regulation Review is created. The workflow is launched from this phase via the Phase Process setting. The review object is available for the submitter to complete and submit.

Replaces the OOTB Review phase. The Delta Regulation Manager analyzes the new regulation to determine if it impacts the company's products or processes. Full Control access is granted to the Delta Regulation Manager role.
Replaces the OOTB Approval phase. The review awaits a formal Yes/No decision from the Delta Regulation Manager. Access is restricted to Read and Download only during this phase.
A new phase added to the Delta life cycle. Indicates that the regulation review has been completed and any required changes have been initiated. The Delta Regulation Manager has Full Control access.

content.text: Phase Configuration

Each life cycle phase can define several properties: the State name that appears on the object, the Phase Process (which workflow to trigger when an object enters this state), the authorized Roles who can act during the phase, and Access Control settings that determine read, download, modify, and full control permissions. Advanced life cycles can automatically trigger workflows when objects reach specific states, enabling process automation without manual intervention.

Life cycles can automatically trigger workflows when objects reach specific states by setting the Phase Process property. This means a single object creation can kick off an entire automated review process - no manual workflow launch required.

content.prompt: Arrange the Delta Regulation Review life cycle phases in the correct order:

keyTakeaways: Life cycle templates define the states, authorized roles, and access control settings for each phase a change object passes through
The Delta Regulation Review LC modifies the OOTB Design Review LC by renaming Review to In Analysis, Approval to Pending Approval, and adding a Resolved phase
Each phase can specify a Phase Process (workflow), authorized roles, and access control permissions (read, download, modify, full control)
Life cycles, workflows, and team templates are the three pillars of change management, connected by Object Initialization Rules

topics.title: Object Initialization Rules

content.text: Object Initialization Rules (OIRs) are the glue that connects workflows to life cycles. When a user creates a change object, the OIR determines which life cycle template, workflow template, team template, and versioning scheme are automatically assigned. Without OIRs, you would have to manually associate every new object with the correct templates - a process that would be error-prone and inconsistent.
What OIRs Control

rows: Life Cycle Assignment
Determines which life cycle template is applied when the object is created
Delta Review objects get the Delta Regulation Review LC
Workflow Association
Specifies which workflow template is launched at the initial life cycle phase
Delta Regulation Review Workflow is triggered at the Open phase
Team Template
Assigns a team template that defines roles and membership for the object
Delta Review Team with Delta Regulation Manager role
Versioning Scheme
Controls how the object is versioned (e.g., revision labels, iteration scheme)
Standard versioning with numeric iterations

content.text: OIR Configuration Details

OIRs are written in XML and assigned to a specific object class. They establish default values and display constraints for object attributes upon creation. Rules can be defined at three context levels: Site (global), Organization, and Application (product or library). PTC recommends turning off the OOTB rules and defining custom rules that match your tailored change process. The OIR Manager is accessed from the Utilities page of any context.

PTC recommends creating and managing OIRs at the organization level rather than at the site or application level. Organization-level rules provide a consistent baseline across all products and libraries within that organization, while still allowing application-level overrides when a specific product needs different behavior.

To create a custom OIR, the typical approach is: (1) download an existing composite rule XML from the OIR Manager, (2) modify the life cycle name and other settings in the XML, and (3) upload it as a new OIR assigned to your custom object type. For the Delta Review process, the OIR connects the Delta Regulation Review Workflow to the Delta Regulation Review LC, ensuring that every new Delta Review object automatically receives the correct workflow and life cycle.

content.prompt: Match each OIR concept to its description:

pairs.right: Establishes default values and template assignments when an object is created
The file format used to define OIR settings that can be downloaded and modified
Recommended context level that provides consistency across all products and libraries
Administration tool accessed from the Utilities page of any context

keyTakeaways: OIRs connect workflows to life cycles by automatically assigning the correct templates when a change object is created
OIRs control four key settings: life cycle assignment, workflow association, team template, and versioning scheme
OIR rules are written in XML, assigned to a specific object class, and can be defined at Site, Organization, or Application level
PTC recommends managing OIRs at the organization level for consistency, and turning off OOTB rules in favor of custom rules

topics.title: Create Delta Workflow

content.text: In this exercise, you will copy the OOTB Design Review Workflow and modify it to create the Delta Regulation Review Workflow. You will rename activities, change routing events from Approve/Reject to Yes/No, update responsible roles, and configure Set State robots to align with the custom life cycle states.
Ensure you are logged in to the Windchill training environment and have navigated to Organizations > PTC Power Equipment. You will need the Delta Regulation Manager role created in a previous module.

content.title: Create Delta Workflow
content.objective: Copy and modify the Design Review Workflow to create the Delta Regulation Review Workflow with custom activities, routing, and a Change Notice segment.

steps.action: Open the Navigator and go to Organizations > PTC Power Equipment > Templates.
steps.detail: This is where workflow templates are stored at the organization level.

steps.action: Select Workflow Templates from the Pick a View menu.
steps.detail: Filters the templates table to show only workflow templates.

steps.action: Right-click the Design Review Workflow and select Save As.
steps.detail: Always copy an existing workflow rather than modifying the original OOTB template.

steps.action: In the Save As dialog, type "Delta Reg Review WF" in the Name field.
steps.detail: The name should reflect the Delta Drills regulation review process.

steps.action: Click OK and note the new template appears in the table, checked out to you.
steps.detail: The template is checked out for editing after being copied.

steps.action: Right-click Delta Reg Review WF and click Edit, then maximize the window.
steps.detail: Opens the visual workflow editor.

steps.action: Click the word "Approve" below the Activity icon on the far right of the workflow.
steps.detail: This opens the activity properties for the approval step.

steps.action: Change the Name field to "Decision Required".
steps.detail: The Delta process uses a Yes/No decision rather than Approve/Reject.

steps.action: In the Responsible Role field, select "Delta Regulation Manager".
steps.detail: Assigns the decision to the custom role you created earlier.

steps.action: Select the Activity tab and set Template Name to "Delta Regulation - Review".
steps.detail: Associates the activity with the correct task form template.

steps.action: In the Instructions field, type "Does the new regulation require a change to our product or process? Yes/No."
steps.detail: Clear instructions help the assigned user understand what decision is needed.

steps.action: Clear the Signing Required checkbox.
steps.detail: Signing required forces credential re-entry which is not needed for this review.

steps.action: Select the Participants tab, remove "Approver", click Roles, select "Delta Regulation Manager", and click Add.
steps.detail: Ensures the correct role receives the task.

tasks.title: Modify Routing and Set State Robots

steps.action: Select the Routing tab and delete the routing events Approve and Reject.
steps.detail: The Delta process uses Yes/No instead of Approve/Reject.

steps.action: Type "Yes" and press Enter, then type "No" to create new routing events.
steps.detail: These match the decision options in the activity instructions.

steps.action: In the Routing/Tallying Expression, change "Approve" to "Yes" and "Reject" to "No" (two occurrences each).
steps.detail: Updates the tallying logic to use the new routing event names.

steps.action: Click Check Syntax to verify, then click OK to close the dialog.
steps.detail: Always verify routing expressions before saving.

steps.action: Update the Set State Review robot: change Name to "Set State In Analysis" and set Specific State to "In Analysis".
steps.detail: Matches the life cycle states you will create later.

steps.action: Update the Set State Approval robot: change Name to "Set State Pending Approval" and set Specific State to "Pending Approval".
steps.detail: Aligns the workflow states with the custom life cycle.

steps.action: Add an End icon below the Set State Rejected robot and connect the arrow from Set State Rejected to it.
steps.detail: Ends the workflow if the regulation review is rejected.

steps.action: Save the workflow and close the editor, then check in the template.
steps.detail: Check-in makes the template available for use.

keyTakeaways: Always copy an OOTB workflow using Save As before making any modifications
Activity node modifications include changing the name, responsible role, template, instructions, and participants
Routing events and tallying expressions must be updated together to keep workflow logic consistent
Set State robots must be renamed and reconfigured to match the states defined in your custom life cycle

topics.title: Modify Change Notice Segment

content.text: In this exercise, you will import a workflow that includes a Change Notice segment and modify it to work with the Delta Regulation Review process. The Change Notice segment extends the review workflow by enabling the creation of a Delta Change Notice when a regulation requires product or process changes.
You will need the Partial Delta Regulation Review.zip file provided with the training materials. Ensure you are in the Workflow Templates view under Organizations > PTC Power Equipment > Templates.

content.title: Modify Change Notice Segment
content.objective: Import a workflow with a Change Notice segment and modify it to work with the Delta Regulation Review process.

tasks.title: Import Workflow with Change Notice Section

steps.action: In the Templates table, ensure Workflow Templates is selected in the Pick a View list.
steps.detail: You need to be viewing workflow templates to import one.

steps.action: Click the Actions menu and select Import.
steps.detail: Import allows loading a pre-configured workflow template from a file.

steps.action: Click Choose File, select the Partial Delta Regulation Review.zip file, and click Open.
steps.detail: This file contains the workflow with the Change Notice segment already attached.

steps.action: Click OK to import the Delta Regulation Review Workflow.
steps.detail: The imported workflow includes both the review portion and the Change Notice segment.

steps.action: Right-click Delta Regulation Review Workflow and click Edit, then maximize the window.
steps.detail: Opens the complete workflow with the CN segment for editing.

steps.action: Click "Create Delta Change Notice" text and select the Activity tab.
steps.detail: Configures which template is used for creating the change notice.

steps.action: Select "Create Delta Change Notice" in the Template Name field and click OK.
steps.detail: Associates the activity with the correct template.

steps.action: Click the "Change Request Resolved Notify By E-mail" robot text.
steps.detail: This notification robot needs to reference the Delta process instead of generic change request.

steps.action: Change the name to "Delta Regulation Review Resolved" and set Responsible Role to "Delta Regulation Manager".
steps.detail: Updates the notification to use the correct role and naming.

steps.action: Update the Description and Message tab to replace "Change Request" with "Delta Regulation Review".
steps.detail: Ensures notification content correctly identifies the process.

steps.action: Save the workflow, close the editor, and check in the template.
steps.detail: Check-in makes the modified workflow available for use.

keyTakeaways: Workflow templates can be imported from ZIP files to add pre-configured segments like Change Notice creation
The Change Notice segment extends the review workflow to enable downstream change implementation
Notification robots must be updated to reference the correct process name and responsible role
Always check in workflow templates after editing to make them available for use

topics.title: Create Life Cycle and OIR

content.text: In this exercise, you will create the Delta Regulation Review life cycle by copying and modifying the OOTB Design Review Life Cycle, then create an Object Initialization Rule to connect the custom workflow and life cycle. Once complete, every new Delta Review object will automatically receive the correct workflow, life cycle, and team configuration.
Ensure you are in the Templates section under Organizations > PTC Power Equipment. You will need the Delta Regulation Review Workflow created in the previous exercises.

content.title: Create Life Cycle and OIR
content.objective: Create the Delta Regulation Review life cycle and an Object Initialization Rule to connect the workflow and life cycle.

tasks.title: Create Delta Regulation Review Life Cycle

steps.action: In the Templates table, select Life Cycle Templates from the Pick a View menu.
steps.detail: Switches to viewing life cycle templates.

steps.action: Right-click Design Review Life Cycle, select Save As, and name it "Delta Regulation Review LC".
steps.detail: Copies the OOTB life cycle as a starting point.

steps.action: Right-click the new life cycle and select Edit.
steps.detail: Opens the life cycle editor.

steps.action: Select the Open phase, go to the Workflow tab, and set Phase Process to "Delta Regulation Review Workflow".
steps.detail: Associates your custom workflow with the initial phase.

steps.action: Select the Review phase, change State to "In Analysis", update Roles to "Delta Regulation Manager", and set Full Control access.
steps.detail: Configures who can act during the analysis phase.

steps.action: Select the Approval phase, change State to "Pending Approval", update Roles to "Delta Regulation Manager" with Read and Download access.
steps.detail: Restricts modifications during the approval phase.

steps.action: Click New Phase, set State to "Resolved", add "Delta Regulation Manager" role with Full Control access.
steps.detail: Adds the final state for completed reviews.

steps.action: Click Save & Close, then check in the life cycle.
steps.detail: Makes the life cycle available for use.

tasks.title: Create Delta Review Object Initialization Rule

steps.action: Navigate to Organizations > PTC Power Equipment > Utilities and select Object Initialization Rules Administration.
steps.detail: OIRs are managed from the Utilities page.

steps.action: Click the Download Composite Rule icon, find and select "Delta Review", and click OK.
steps.detail: Downloads the existing OIR XML as a starting point.

steps.action: Open the downloaded rule.xml file in a text editor.
steps.detail: The XML file contains the life cycle assignment that needs to be updated.

steps.action: In line 16, replace "Basic" with "Delta Regulation Review LC" and save as "Delta OIR".
steps.detail: This tells the OIR to use your custom life cycle instead of the default.

steps.action: Click New Object Initialization Rule, name it "Delta Review OIR", and select "Delta Review" as the object type.
steps.detail: Creates a new OIR for your custom change object.

steps.action: Choose the modified Delta OIR.xml file and click OK.
steps.detail: Uploads the XML that connects the Delta workflow to the Delta life cycle.

keyTakeaways: Life cycles are created by copying an OOTB template and modifying the phases, roles, and access control settings
Each life cycle phase can specify a Phase Process workflow, authorized roles, and granular access permissions
OIRs are created by downloading an existing composite rule XML, modifying the life cycle reference, and uploading it as a new rule
The OIR connects the Delta Regulation Review Workflow to the Delta Regulation Review LC, automating template assignment for new objects
